title: Caldera - Credential Search in Files via grep (Linux)
description: Detects use of grep or find to search for credentials stored in configuration and history files. Caldera T1552.001 emulation runs grep -r with keywords like password, passwd, secret, token, and apikey across common configuration paths. Complements the SSRF/metadata credential rules with file-system-based grep detection.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Secure Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Syslog Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Linux Audit Logs') and (((msg like '*grep -r password*' or msg like '*grep -ri password*' or msg like '*grep -r passwd*' or msg like '*grep -ri passwd*' or msg like '*grep -r secret*' or msg like '*grep -r apikey*' or msg like '*grep -r api_key*' or msg like '*grep -r token*')) or ((msg like '*grep -r /etc/*' or msg like '*grep -r /home/*' or msg like '*grep -r /root/*' or msg like '*grep -r /var/www/*' or msg like '*grep -r /opt/*')) or ((msg like '*cat /root/.bash_history*' or msg like '*cat /home/*' or msg like '*.bash_history*' or msg like '*cat /root/.mysql_history*')) or ((msg like '*cat /root/.netrc*' or msg like '*cat ~/.netrc*' or msg like '*cat /home/*' or msg like '*.netrc*')))
falsepositives: Security hardening scripts searching for credential leaks
